channel.title: Research Resources on IQSS Clone - Harvard Institute for Quantitative Social Science
channel.description: Recent content in Research Resources on IQSS Clone - Harvard Institute for Quantitative Social Science

item.title: Available Software
item.description:
We work to provide all the software you might need to get your work done. The following software is installed on lab workstations:

Adobe Creative Suite

Adobe Acrobat DC, After Effects 2025, Animate 2024, Audition 2025, Bridge 2025, Character Animator 2025, Dimension, Dreamweaver 2021, Illustrator 2025, InCopy 2025, InDesign 2025, Lightroom CC, Media Encoder 2025, Photoshop 2025, Prelude 2023, Premiere Pro 2025, Premiere Rush, XD

Statistical & Data Analysis

  • IBM SPSS 29
  • Matlab 2024b
  • Mplus 7.11
  • R / RStudio
  • SAS 9.4
  • Stata 18
  • StatTransfer 17
  • Tableau Desktop / Tableau Prep Builder
  • Wolfram Mathematica
  • Anaconda Python

GIS & Spatial Analysis

  • ArcGIS Pro
  • QGIS
  • GeoDa
  • Geographic Calculator
  • Global Mapper
  • Google Earth Pro
  • ERDAS Imagine 2023 / ER Mapper 2023
  • Envi 5.7 + IDL 8.9
  • FME Form 2024
  • TerrSet
  • eCognition Developer
  • Knime

Qualitative Research

  • Atlas.TI
  • NVivo

3D Design & Visualization

  • Autodesk 3ds Max, AutoCAD, Maya, Revit
  • ChimeraX
  • Enscape
  • Lumion 2024
  • Meshlab
  • Rhino 8 / VRay 6 for Rhino
  • Sketchup 2024
  • Slicer

Video & Audio Production

  • Final Cut Pro
  • Compressor
  • Motion
  • Logic Pro X
  • MainStage 3
  • Garageband
  • iMovie
  • VLC

Programming & Development

  • Anaconda Python
  • Iron Python
  • Node.js
  • Emacs
  • Notepad++
  • Sublime Text
  • XCode
  • ConTEXT
  • PuTTY / SecureCRT
  • FileZilla

Writing & Reference

  • Microsoft Office 2021 (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Access, Publisher, Visio, Project Pro, OneNote)
  • Apple iWork (Pages, Numbers, Keynote)
  • EndNote
  • Mendeley Reference Manager
  • Zotero
  • LyX
  • MiKTeX / TeXmaker / TeXstudio

Other Tools

  • Abbyy Finereader 15
  • CrimeStat
  • Dropbox / Google Drive
  • Firefox / Google Chrome
  • Gephi
  • The UnArchiver

Software requests: iqsslabs@harvard.edu

item.title: Client Support Services
item.description:
Get Help From Client Support Services

We’re here to help you with your technological needs including, but not limited to, computer, smartphone, tablet and printer issues.

Available to assist you:
Monday–Friday: 9am – 5pm
Location: CGIS Knafel Building, 1737 Cambridge Street, Cambridge MA

We strive to provide prompt, high-quality service to the research students, staff, and faculty within the CGIS community.

Self Service Resources

Connecting via VPN

Use VPN to access your network space while off campus. Set up Okta, install Harvard's VPN client from vpn.harvard.edu, and connect using the address vpn.harvard.edu/gencom.

item.title: Computer Lab FAQs
item.description:
Can I eat in the lab?

Food is not permitted in the labs. Please stay hydrated but dispose of any food outside of the lab to keep the area free of pests that also love to consume wires.

Can I print to lab printers from my personal computer?

Yes. You must select hmdc-crimson-print to be able to print to our printers. Instructions are available for both Windows and macOS laptops.

item.title: Data Science Services
item.description:
Advisory Services

Consulting

We provide short-term support (up to 3 hours per publication) in the form of advice.

Collaboration

We provide extended support over the lifecycle of a research project by embedding a data science specialist in your research team.

Analytical Tool Building

We can develop R code to implement your new statistical method or to automate part of your analysis pipeline.

Training Services

  • Workshop Materials — Self-contained workshops in Python, R, Stata, and more
  • Tutorials — Step-by-step guides for LaTeX, Git, causal inference, web scraping, and more
  • Related Resources — Additional data science resources across IQSS and Harvard University

Impact

  • Research Output — Publications and dissertations supported by Data Science Services

Our Team

Steven Worthington

Director of Data Science Services. At IQSS since 2011. PhD in Biological Anthropology from NYU.

item.title: Extraordinary Claims, Extraordinary Evidence
item.description:
Program Overview

IQSS invites Harvard social science tenure-track faculty to collaborate on research that challenges dominant scientific, political, or cultural assumptions. The program provides tailored support including high-level data expertise, infrastructure, and funding to help investigators collect the extraordinary evidence required to move the scientific community’s dominant position the farthest distance.

What is an “Extraordinary Claim”?

An extraordinary claim is an empirical claim that is unusually distant from the current dominant view of the scientific community.

item.title: How to Apply — For Faculty
item.description:
Faculty Affiliates: Apply for Funding

Faculty seeking URS funding should complete the URS Grant Application survey form. After review, a URS Program administrator will follow up.

Submission Deadlines

URS Funding is currently on hold. We hope to re-open applications in the future.

How It Works

  1. Fill out the grant application form
  2. Wait for administrative review
  3. Receive contact from a URS administrator

After Receiving Funding

After receiving funding, faculty have two options:

item.title: How to Apply — For Students
item.description:
For Students: Get Experience

Interested undergraduates should visit the Current Opportunities section for projects needing researchers. To apply, students should send a resume and cover letter with relevant qualifications to the contact listed under the project description.

Even without listed opportunities, students can reach out directly to faculty whose research interests them to inform them about URS. Once a faculty member receives grant approval, they work with IQSS administrators to hire undergraduates.

item.title: IQSS Computer Labs
item.description:
Who Can Access?

The labs serve faculty, visiting scholars, post-docs, students, and staff. They use University active directory accounts that need activation. New patrons can sign up via the access request form.

Current/active MIT affiliates who do not have University active directory accounts may sign up for lab access — IQSS will sponsor a University active directory account; the process may take several days for new users.

The labs are open to all active members of the Harvard and MIT Community.

item.title: rapidPeer
item.description:
Why rapidPeer?

Boost Your Publication Odds

Get a professional-quality review without using a journal submission.

Accelerate Your Publishing Timeline

Catch flaws before they do; submit with peace of mind and get published faster.

Save Time and Money

Get early-stage feedback and head in the right direction from the start.

How Does It Work?

  1. Submit your research — Upload your paper, grant, or preregistration plan
  2. Select potential reviewers — Choose from field-specific experts
  3. Quality peer review delivered in 30 days — Receive constructive, detailed feedback
  4. Revise and submit for publication — Strengthen your work before journal submission

Submit Your Paper

item.title: Related Resources
item.description:
IQSS Resources

  • Data Science Resources at IQSS — Listing of Data Science resources available at IQSS
  • rapidPeer — Fast, constructive, double-blind peer review by a leading external scholar
  • Extraordinary Claims, Extraordinary Evidence — Supercharged research support for projects with potential to move the needle
  • Center for Geographic Analysis Training — GIS workshops
  • Harvard Dataverse — A platform for sharing, publishing, and archiving research data
  • Program on Survey Research — Support with survey theory and practice

Harvard Research Support

  • FAS Research Computing — Computing cluster, support and training funded by FAS
  • Research Computing Services, Harvard Business School — Support for statistical analysis and computing for HBS researchers
  • Research Support Services, Harvard Graduate School of Education — Research software training (R, Stata, Mplus, NVivo, Atlas.ti) and consulting
  • Digital Arts & Humanities at Harvard — Support and consultation for digital humanities research
  • Harvard Chan Bioinformatics Core — Consultations on research computing, bioinformatics and computational biology
  • Copyright First Responders, Harvard Library — Have a copyright question? Ask the copyright first responders!
  • Harvard Digital Scholarship Support Group — Digital support for scholars

Software and Tools

  • Harvard FAS Software Download — Download Stata and SPSS (for FAS affiliates only)
  • CRAN — Download R
  • IDRE UCLA — Tutorials on R, Stata, SPSS, SAS and more

item.title: Research Computing at IQSS
item.description:
Get Started

Researchers access the FAS Research Computing clusters for social science computing and analysis — a centralized place for data storage and collaborative analysis.

Get an IQSS Cannon Account

Office Hours

Having technical issues using FASRC? General questions? Drop in for FASRC virtual office hours, open to all users and labs.

Learn More About Office Hours

General Inquiries: gen_inquiries@iq.harvard.edu

Phone: 617-496-2450

Address: 1737 Cambridge Street, CGIS Knafel Building, Room K350, Cambridge, MA 02138

item.title: Research Output
item.description:
We are always very interested to hear about research that has been impacted by Data Science Services. If your work has benefited from our support, please let us know.

Acknowledging DSS

If Data Science Services contributed to your research, we ask that you acknowledge us in your publications. A suggested acknowledgment:

“The authors wish to acknowledge the support of IQSS Data Science Services at Harvard University.”

Selected Publications (2018)

  • Worthington, S. — Hypodontia prevalence in permanent teeth, systematic review/meta-analysis. Scientific Reports
  • Park, D.S., Worthington, S., & Zhenxiang, X. — Taxon sampling effects on community phylogenetic diversity. Molecular Ecology
  • Yeomans, M., Stewart, B., Mavon, K., et al. — Civic mission of MOOCs. International Journal of AI in Education
  • Liebman, J. & Mahoney, N. — Expiring budgets and wasteful year-end spending. American Economic Review 107(11)
  • Ban, P., Fouirnaies, A., Hall, A.B. & Snyder, J. — How newspapers reveal political power. Political Science Research and Methods
  • Zhang, Letian — Racial bias and repeated interaction between NBA coaches and players. Administrative Science Quarterly 62(4)
  • Dillon, M.R. et al. — Preschool intervention durably enhances intuitive mathematics. Science 357(6346)
  • Blake, P.R., McAuliffe, K. et al. — Ontogeny of fairness in seven societies. Nature 528
  • Baldwin, M.W. et al. — Evolution of taste perception in hummingbirds. Science 345

Selected Publications (2017)

  • Wrangham, R.W., Worthington, S. et al. — Response to chimpanzee culture beyond matrilineal family units. Current Biology 27
  • Wallace, I.J., Worthington, S. et al. — Knee osteoarthritis doubled in prevalence since mid-20th century. PNAS 114(35)
  • Huang, K. et al. — Question-asking increases liking. Journal of Personality and Social Psychology 113(3)
  • Mehr, S.A. et al. — Genomic imprinting implicated in psychology of music. Psychological Science 28(10)
  • Nagase, N., Brinton, M. — Gender division of labor and second births in Japan. Demographic Research 36

Selected Publications (2016)

  • Cao, J., & Banaji, M.R. — Base rate principle and fairness principle in social judgment. PNAS 113(27)
  • Snyder, J., Ban, P. and Llaudet, E. — Challenger quality and incumbency advantage. Legislative Studies Quarterly 41(1)
  • Carter, K. and Worthington, S. — Evolution of anthropoid molar proportions. BMC Evolutionary Biology 16:110

Selected Publications (2015)

  • Blake, P.R., McAuliffe, K. et al. — Ontogeny of fairness in seven societies. Nature 528
  • Glowacki, L. and Wrangham, R.W. — Warfare and reproductive success in tribal population. PNAS 112(2)
  • Jamal, A. et al. — Anti-Americanism or anti-interventionism in Arabic Twitter. Perspectives on Politics 13(1)
  • Tingley, D. et al. — Computer-assisted text analysis for comparative politics. Political Analysis 23(2)

Dissertations Supported

Data Science Services has supported over 30 dissertations across Harvard schools including Sociology, Government, Human Evolutionary Biology, Economics, Public Health, the Kennedy School, the Business School, and MIT.

item.title: Sponsored Research Services
item.description:
IQSS Research Administration

The Sponsored Research Administration Team at IQSS is dedicated to supporting social scientists’ research by delivering services across the full lifecycle of sponsored projects.

The team consists of experienced sponsored research administrators offering both pre-award and post-award support. They primarily serve social scientists in the Faculty of Arts and Sciences (FAS) and across the University, assisting with proposal development and submission, grants management, and reporting. Their goal is to provide outstanding service to IQSS-affiliated investigators with all of their sponsored research needs.

item.title: Training & Workshops
item.description:
Workshops Hosted at IQSS in Collaboration with Other Departments

Applied Statistics Workshop

A weekly, cross-disciplinary tour of Harvard’s statistical innovations and applications.

Learn more about the Applied Statistics Workshop

Tech Science Seminars

Weekly series on Technology Science from expert researchers, public interest groups, and others on the social impact of technology.

Learn more about Tech Science Seminars

Alberto Alesina Seminar on Political Economy

A seminar series on formal and quantitative political research that integrates the study of economics and politics.

item.title: Tutorials
item.description:
LaTeX and Writing Tools

  • LaTeX Applications: A Beginner’s Guide — Covers recommended LaTeX editing options including Overleaf, VSCode, and Emacs, plus tools like GitHub and Copilot.
  • Overleaf: A Beginner’s Guide — Steps for signing up, editing documents, and collaboration in Overleaf.
  • Visual Studio Code: A Beginner’s Guide — Steps for installation and importing recommended settings, keybindings, and shortcuts.
  • Emacs: A Beginner’s Guide — Steps for installation, importing configuration, and common functions for LaTeX editing.

Version Control

  • Git and GitHub: A Beginner’s Guide — Steps for creating and cloning repositories, and using the command line.
  • GitHub Pages: A Beginner’s Guide — Steps for creating a personal website, downloading and customizing templates.

Data Science Methods

  • Rbuild: Create Your Own Packages in R — Best practices for R package development and version control using RStudio.
  • Matching and Weighting for Causal Inference — Covers propensity score analysis methods for adjusting confounding in observational studies. Includes R examples.
  • Web-scraping with Selenium Python — Guide to scraping both static and dynamic websites with Selenium Python.

item.title: Undergraduate Research Scholars
item.description:
Program Benefits

For Faculty

Get funding to hire Undergraduate Research Scholars as research assistants for your project.

For Students

Gain valuable experience as a paid research assistant on new and exciting research projects led by Harvard faculty.

How to Apply

For Faculty

Applying for URS funding is simple and will benefit both you and the undergraduates you hire.

For Students

Students wishing to participate in the URS program can either apply for jobs on the opportunities page, or contact a faculty member they wish to work with.

item.title: Workshop Materials
item.description:
We offer a series of data analysis and programming workshop materials to members of the Harvard and MIT communities, as well as to the public. Each workshop is self-contained and can be studied at one’s own pace.

Python Workshop Materials

Python Introduction

This hands-on workshop introduces the basic elements of Python by working through an example of how to analyze text data. Appropriate for those with little or no prior experience.
